subject: Chemistry
title: Herd Immunity: Chemistry Meets Math
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Herd Immunity: Chemistry Meets Math.

hook.text: When enough people are vaccinated, even unvaccinated people are protected. The disease can't find enough hosts to spread.

buildup.text: Each disease has a threshold. Measles needs 95% vaccination because one infected person can infect 12-18 others.

discovery.text: The formula is simple: threshold = 1 − (1/R₀), where R₀ is the average number of people one sick person infects.

twist.text: When vaccination rates drop below the threshold, outbreaks return. Measles came back in 2019 after years of declining vaccination.

climax.text: Herd immunity protects babies too young for vaccines and people with immune disorders who can't be vaccinated.

punchline.text: Vaccinate enough people and the chain breaks. Math protects the vulnerable.

quiz.question: Why does measles require 95% vaccination for herd immunity?
options: Because measles is so contagious that one person can infect 12-18 others
Because the measles vaccine is only 95% effective
Because 5% of the population is naturally immune to measles
